programme.desc: Everything is bigger in Texas, including the stories. With the whitetail rut in the midwest winding down, the Winchester Life Crew of Chris Keefer, Casey Keefer, Nikki Boxler and Paul Sawyer head south to the Texas Panhandle with Winchesters in hand hoping to wrangle in a few Texas-sized giants.

programme.title: Winchester Life
programme.desc: The positive impact made by the shooting sports and recreational shooting to people across our country can’t be measured in just one story, so we have three. In Florida, Olympian Dania Vizzi trains at her home shooting facility and talk about how she went from ballerina to one of the best shotgun shooters in the world. In Nebraska, firearms instructor Thea Van Syoc has used the disciplines of competitive pistol shooting and training to help shape her life. Finally we’ll take a look into the National African American Gun Association and learn how one day at the range for Phillip Smith created the fastest growing firearm organization in the United States.

programme.title: Winchester Life

We’ll also head to Missouri and Texas to chase whitetails with Chris Keefer and Paul Sawyer.

programme.title: Springtime in the Rockies (1937)
programme.desc: A singing cowboy heads to the Rockies where he becomes involved in a dispute between ranchers and troublemakers threatening the peace of the region. With courage, quick thinking, and a few songs along the way, he works to restore order and protect those in danger.

programme.title: Spook Town (1944)
programme.desc: A government agent travels to a remote town rumored to be haunted, where a series of mysterious events are being used to frighten residents away from valuable land. As he investigates, he uncovers a scheme by outlaws exploiting superstition to carry out their plans, and works to expose the truth and restore order.

programme.title: Joshua - 1979
programme.desc: A hardened Civil War veteran, played by Fred Williamson, returns home to find his family brutally murdered by outlaws. Driven by vengeance, he sets out across the frontier on a relentless hunt for the killers, confronting violence, betrayal, and his own past along the way

programme.title: The Feud of the Trail
programme.desc: A man who's a dead ringer for the leader of an outlaw gang kills the gang leader, then takes his place to try to bring the gang to justice.

programme.title: Blue Steel
programme.desc: U.S. marshal John Carruthers observes a robbery and Sheriff Jake thinks he may be the culprit. Meanwhile the town's leading citizen is planning to rob everybody blind.

programme.title: Sagebrush Trail
programme.desc: A man framed for murder escapes prison and goes west, where he joins a gang with the real killer involved.

programme.title: The Law of 45's 1935
programme.desc: In The Law of 45’s, Western star Guinn ‘Big Boy’ Williams blazes onto the screen as Tucson Smith, one of the famed Three Mesquiteers. When land-grabbing crooks terrorize ranchers, Tucson and his partner Stony Brooke (Al St. John) saddle up to take a stand. With blazing six-guns, quick-draw justice, and plenty of frontier grit, this action-packed adventure marks the beginning of the legendary Mesquiteers series.

programme.title: Fistful of Lead
programme.desc: Four of the West's most infamous outlaws carry out a daring bank heist in the gold-rush town, Bath Water. As the posse takes chase, things take a turn for the worse, as the bandits realize they've been double-crossed - but by who?

programme.title: Metroparks: Endless Experiences
programme.desc: One of America’s premier metropolitan park systems, the Huron-Clinton Metroparks have served the people of Southeast Michigan since 1940. Managed by the Huron-Clinton Metropolitan Authority, the Metroparks are made up of 13 properties in Livingston, Macomb, Oakland, Washtenaw, and Wayne counties. Available activities include fishing, swimming, boating, hiking, nature study, biking, golf, winter sports and more. The Metroparks also provide educational resources on science, nature, history and the environment.
